revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:五十猛神]][[simple:Isotakeru-no-kami]][[zh:五十猛神]] {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | cult_centre = {{ill|Watatsu Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsu Shrine|qq=}}
{{ill|Itakiso shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Itakiso shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakiso shrine|qq=}}
{{ill|Takase Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takase Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takase Shrine|qq=}} | father = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] | image = File:Isotakeru shinbutsu-zue.jpg | caption = Depiction in shinbutsu-zue |name=Isotakeru-no-kami|god_of=trees| gender = {{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}} | parents = {{ill|Susanoo|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} }} {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|qid=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|qid=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-kami|ja|五十猛神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|en:draft|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|qq=|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}'''|五十猛神|}}, also read as Itakeru-no-mikoto, is a [[kami]] and the son of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] and the god of forests and forestry he is worshipped in {{ill|Itakiso shrine|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Itakiso shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakiso shrine|qq=}}{{Cite web|date=2016-10-09|title=Itakiso-jinja Shrine|url=https://www.mysecretwakayama.com/introduce/388/|access-date=2024-03-20|website=my secret Wakayama|language=en}}{{Cite web|title=Itakiso-Jinja Shrine|url=https://itakiso-jinja.net/m/about_en.html|access-date=2024-03-20|website=itakiso-jinja.net}} and at {{ill|Takase Shrine|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Takase Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takase Shrine|qq=}}{{Cite web|title=Isotakeru • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/isotakeru/|access-date=2023-10-20|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}{{Cite web|title=Japan Shinto Kami Gods {{!}} Iso Takeru 五十猛神 {{!}} Rods Shinto|url=https://www.rodsshinto.com/iso-takeru|access-date=2023-10-20|website=shintoshrines|language=en}}{{Cite journal|last=Aragona|first=Jared|title=from Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Vol. 1|url=https://open.maricopa.edu/worldmythologyvolume1godsandcreation/chapter/from-nihongi-chronicles-of-japan-from-the-earliest-times-to-a-d-697-vol-1/|language=en}}{{Cite web|title=Magatsubi no Kami and Motoori Norinaga's Theology|url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/cpjr/kami/ueda.html|access-date=2023-10-20|website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}{{Cite web|date=2023-05-25|title=Isotakeru {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230525152851/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9391|access-date=2024-03-24|website=web.archive.org}} He is only called Isotakeru in the [[Nihon Shoki]]. He is called {{ill|Oyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyabiko|qq=}} in the [[Kojiki]]. According to the [[Nihon_Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] and his son Isotakeru had been driven out of the land of [[Takamagahara|Takamagahara]] and had landed in {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|qid=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}}. However, they did not like that land and said it was inhospitable so they travelled by boat to [[Izumo_Province|Izumo]]. There, Susanoo handed over the seeds of the tree he had brought from Takamagahara to Isotakeru and ordered him to sow the seeds all over Japan. Isotakeru, along with his younger sisters {{ill|Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime|ja|オオヤツヒメ・ツマツヒメ}}, began to sow seeds all over Japan, turning the entire country into forests, finally arriving in the "country of trees", or {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|qid=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}}..{{cite book|title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido|last1=Yoshiki|first1=Emi|date=2007|publisher=PHP Institute|isbn=978-4569669304|language=Japanese}} A different version of the legend suggests that Susanoo, envious of the prosperity of the land of Han, extracted numerous hairs from his body, which he transformed into a vast assortment of trees. This act was intended to bestow the wealth of timber upon the people. Following this event, his three offspring, Isotakeru, Oyatsuhime, and Tsumatsuhime, took it upon themselves to scatter the seeds of these trees throughout Japan. Subsequently, they settled in the region of Kii. Different hairs from different parts of his body produced different species. Much later in the Kojiki during the story of the {{ill|Hare of Inaba|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hare of Inaba|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hare of Inaba|qq=}} he protected [[Ōkuninushi|Onamuchi]] when he was being pursued by his eighty brothers.{{cite book|title=Kojiki|last1=Philippi|first1=Donald L.|date=2015|publisher=Princeton University Press|pages=96–97}}Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj029.htm SECT. XXII.—Mount Tema.] {{ill|Itakiso shrine|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Itakiso shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakiso shrine|qq=}} was originally located on what is the site of [[Hinokuma_Shrine|Hinokuma Shrine]] but during the reign of {{ill|Emperor_Suinin|en|Emperor_Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}} was relocated to a place called "Anomori" near its current location, and then relocated again to its present site in 713. ==References== {{reflist}} {{Isotakeru Faith}} {{translated page|ja|五十猛神|version=103596988|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:五十猛神]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami]] [[ja:Isotakeru-no-Mikoto]] {{Commons category|Itakeru}} [[zh:五十猛神]] {{wikidata link|Q10881556}} [[Category:Forestry in Japan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Nature deities]] [[Category:Vegetation gods]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
